wine_museums.name: museumkrems - Weinstadtmuseum
wine_museums.address: Körnermarkt 14, 3500 Krems an der Donau, Austria
wine_museums.opening_hours: Tuesday to Sunday 10:00-18:00 (March-October); 10:00-17:00 (November-February); closed Mondays except public holidays
wine_museums.description: The city museum of Krems housed in a 13th-century Dominican monastery, including a historic wine cellar. Exhibits trace viticulture and the wine trade as the foundation of Krems citizenry from the Middle Ages; works by baroque painter Kremser Schmidt also displayed.

wine_museums.name: Schifffahrtsmuseum Spitz
wine_museums.address: Auf der Wehr 21, 3620 Spitz an der Donau, Austria
wine_museums.museum_type: Danube shipping and wine trade history museum
wine_museums.admission: Standard museum admission; group and off-hours bookings available
wine_museums.description: The Spitz shipping museum in a historic building preserving model ships, horse-drawn towboat equipment and the last functioning 17th-century boat organ. Exhibits document the Danube wine trade that made the Wachau prosperous, including royal barges transporting Wachau wines to Vienna.

wine_museums.name: Wachaumuseum Weißenkirchen
wine_museums.address: Wachaustraße 242, 3610 Weißenkirchen in der Wachau, Austria
wine_museums.museum_type: regional history and cultural museum of the Wachau valley
wine_museums.opening_hours: Contact Gemeinde Weißenkirchen for seasonal hours: +43 (0) 2715 2232
wine_museums.description: Located in the historic Teisenhoferhof in Weißenkirchen, the Wachaumuseum traces the cultural, social and agricultural development of the valley across centuries, with strong emphasis on viticulture and the apricot economy that defines Wachau identity.

verified.address_quoted: Wachaustraße 242 A-3610 Weißenkirchen in der Wachau

wine_museums.name: Weingut Stift Göttweig - Historic Cellar Heritage
wine_museums.museum_type: monastery wine heritage cellar tour and museum-quality exhibits
wine_museums.admission: EUR 1 per tasting sample (waived with purchase); guided experience cellar tours on request
wine_museums.description: The Benedictine Abbey Göttweig has produced wine continuously since 1083. The experience cellar tour explores nine centuries of monastic viticulture, including the 1681-expanded Grand Cellar that once stored hundreds of thousands of litres. A museum-quality living wine heritage destination.

wine_museums.name: Nikolaihof Roman Cellar Heritage
wine_museums.address: Nikolaigasse 3, 3512 Mautern an der Donau, Austria
wine_museums.museum_type: in-situ Roman archaeology and wine history at the oldest winery in Austria
wine_museums.admission: Integrated with Salon 77 tastings and Weinstube visits; cellar heritage viewed during estate visits
wine_museums.description: Nikolaihof stands on the site of a Roman settlement dating to the 1st century AD, with intact Roman cellar foundations visible in the vaulted cave. The Saahs family's guided estate tour covers 2,000 years of uninterrupted Wachau viticulture from Roman Pannonia to Demeter-certified biodynamics.
